problem: Can superconductivity be achieved at room temperature — what material structure?
hypothesis: The key to Can superconductivity be achieved at room temperature — what material structure? is Magnetohydrodynamic flow without moving parts — specifically Magnetohydrodynamic flow without — which would help 8,000,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Materials characterization (XRD, SEM) with 6 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The art of science is asking the right question, not answering. — Max Planck paraphrase
